definition: Clear seeing that is free from confusion and distortion.
notes: The project prefers clarity because it reads naturally in contemporary English and captures the functional opposite of delusion. Non-delusion remains acceptable in more technical doctrinal exposition, especially when the explicit contrast with moha needs to stay visible. The term belongs inside the wholesome-root framework and should be heard as lucid seeing, not mere cleverness.

context_rules.context: In most practical and explanatory contexts
In analytical contexts explicitly contrasting the wholesome and unwholesome roots
context_rules.notes: Use when doctrinal symmetry with moha matters.

context_rules.context: When the passage is naming one of the wholesome roots in relation to discernment
context_rules.notes: Keep the practical luminous quality while preserving its role in the root triad.

example_phrases.notes: The wholesome-root triad in the project's preferred style.
Shows the term in explicit contrastive ethical analysis.
